front: What is Edman degradation and what are its limitations?
back: Edman degradation sequentially removes N-terminal amino acids using phenylisothiocyanate (PITC). Each cycle yields a PTH-amino acid identified by HPLC. Limitations: 1) Fails at modified residues, 2) Poor with N-terminal pyroglutamate, 3) Limited to ~50 cycles, 4) Requires free N-terminus.
